toc.text: I. Common Troubleshooting and Maintenance Manual for the IAS-3120
II. Step-by-Step Removal and Installation of the IAS-3120
IV Specific Maintenance Solutions for Various Issues

details.text: 1.1 When the instrument is returned for repair, unpack and reproduce the client-side fault.

steps.title: Check the Kernel Version of the Instrument

details.text: 2.1 Navigate to System Settings - Advanced Settings - enter the password admin123 - OK - access the system backend, and click on Detailed Information in the upper left of Device Information to check the kernel version.

steps.title: Troubleshoot Issues by Referring to the Common Troubleshooting Manual Based on the Kernel Version

troubleshooting.title: IAS-3120 Common Troubleshooting and Maintenance Manual

items.inspection: Check if the power outlet has electricity and if the adapter outputs 24V

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Measure the Button Switch with a multimeter for continuity. Replace if not conducting.
Check if the peripheral board LED lights up after powering on. Replace if not.
Replace the ARM board
items.maintenance: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients

items.inspection: Measure the button switch with a multimeter for continuity. Replace if not conducting.
Replace the main control board
Replace the screen

items.category: Stuck on Penguin Interface During Boot
items.inspection: Replace the main control board

items.category: Stuck on Penguin Interface During Boot
items.inspection: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients
items.maintenance: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients

items.inspection: Reconnect the XHB cable, apply adhesive, or replace it
items.maintenance: Reconnect the XHB cable, apply adhesive, or replace it

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Visually inspect the 485 communication cable for looseness
Visually inspect the analyzer USB interface for looseness or soldering issues. If such issues are found, replace the analyze mainboard.
Reconnect the USB cable. If the issue persists, replace the USB cable.

items.possibleCause: Malfunctioning USB interface on the peripheral board
items.inspection: Insert a USB drive and check if the USB icon appears. If it does not, replace the peripheral board.
Observe whether the lamp illuminates normally during startup. If it does not, replace the lamp.

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Use a multimeter to measure the output voltage (should be ~11V). If not, replace the lamp board.
During startup, manually rotate the rotary disc and check for torque. If none, replace the peripheral board.
Replace the PD board
Replace the analyzer mainboard
Replace the DMD board
Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients
items.maintenance: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients

items.inspection: Block strong light and check if the fault clears. If not, replace the sensor.

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Reassemble or replace the rotary disc
items.maintenance: Reassemble or replace the rotary disc

items.inspection: During startup, manually rotate the rotary disc and check for torque. If none, replace the peripheral board.
During startup, manually rotate the rotary disc and check for torque. If none, replace the motor board.
Replace the Motor
Observe whether the lamp illuminates normally during startup. If it does not, replace the lamp.
Use a multimeter to measure the output voltage (should be ~11V). If not, replace the lamp board.
Replace the DMD board
Replace the PD board
Replace the analyzer core board
Replace the optical fiber
Perform self-check after a 15-minute warm-up.
Replace the lamp or peripheral board

steps.instruction: Remove 7 screws from the bottom plate
Remove 7 fixing screws from the back panel and detach the back panel
Disconnect the collection button and power button switch cables
Remove 4 hex screws from the front panel, use a blade to cut the sticker on the rotating disk, remove 3 screws from the rotating disk, and detach the rotating disk and screen cover
Remove 4 fixing screws from the front panel to take off the casing (after removing the casing, secure the four corners of the screen with tape to prevent it from falling off)
Reassemble the device by reversing the disassembly steps
Remove 7 screws from the back panel
Disconnect the ribbon cable connected to the button
Remove the switch and install the new switch (reverse the process)
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Disconnect all ribbon cables connected to the peripheral board, then remove the 4 screws securing the peripheral board
Install the new peripheral board, secure it with 4 screws, and reconnect the previously removed ribbon cables to the new peripheral board, then reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Disconnect all ribbon cables connected to the peripheral board, then remove the 4 screws securing the peripheral board
After removing the peripheral board, take out the motor board from the peripheral board
Install the removed motor board onto the new peripheral board
Reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)
Remove the blue ARM board within the circle, insert the new ARM board, and reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)
Remove the blue ARM board within the circle, insert the new ARM board, and reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)
Disconnect the screen ribbon cable and remove the screen
Install the new screen from the front, and connect the ribbon cable to the control main board
Reassemble in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)
Disconnect the screen ribbon cable and remove the screen
Install the new screen from the front, and connect the ribbon cable to the control main board
Reassemble in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Disconnect the 2 ribbon cables on the light source board, remove the 4 screws securing it, and replace it with a new light source board
Reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Disconnect the 40-pin gray ribbon cable connected to the control main board, and replace it with a new cable
Reassemble in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)
Disconnect the screen cable connected to the control main board, remove the central blue ARM board, then loosen the 4 screws securing the control main board and replace it with a new one
Insert the old blue ARM board into the new control main board, then reassemble the control main board in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)
Disconnect the screen cable connected to the control main board, remove the central blue ARM board, then loosen the 4 screws securing the control main board and replace it with a new one
Insert the old blue ARM board into the new control main board, then reassemble the control main board in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Disconnect the spectrum analyzer 485 communication cable, replace it with a new cable (pay attention to the wire sequence)
Reassemble in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: After removing the casing, the spectrum analyzer location is shown in the figure
After removing the casing, remove the 4 screws within the circle to detach the front panel (ensure to use tape to secure the screen during removal to prevent it from falling off)

As shown, use a utility knife to cut the white adhesive connecting the spectrum analyzer, then remove the two screws securing the optical fiber to the spectrum analyzer, and finally remove the two screws securing the spectrum analyzer bracket to the baseplate. Disconnect the USB interface connected to the core board, and remove the spectrum analyzer. Reassemble in reverse order.

As shown, first disconnect the ribbon cables from the core board and PD board (ensure the blue side of the ribbon cable faces outward), then remove the screws securing the PD board and core board, replace the PD board and core board. Reassemble in reverse order.

left: Two screws for the PD board
Four screws for the core board
right: Remove the wiring of the core board

steps.instruction: As shown, first disconnect the ribbon cable from the DMD board (ensure the blue side of the ribbon cable faces outward), then remove the screws securing the DMD board, replace the DMD board. Reassemble in reverse order.
Reassemble in reverse order
Remove 7 screws from the back panel
As shown, remove 4 screws on each side of the lamp holder assembly for replacement, paying attention to the green lamp wire connection
Reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Remove the 4 screws securing the top cover plate within the circle

Disconnect all ribbon cables and optical fibers connected to the top cover plate, remove the top cover plate, remove the 4 screws securing the motor, and replace the motor. Pay attention to the direction of the motor's connection port.
Reassemble the device in reverse order
Remove 7 screws from the back panel
Disconnect both ends of the XHB cable, replace it with a new XHB cable
Disconnect both ends of the FC-20 cable, replace it with a new FC-20 cable
Disconnect both ends of the USB cable, replace it with a new USB cable
After completion, reinstall the back panel
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Remove the 4 screws securing the top cover plate within the circle
Disconnect all ribbon cables and optical fibers connected to the top cover plate, and remove the top cover plate

Remove the Hall sensor, and when reinstalling, push the Hall sensor to the farthest side away from the lamp holder assembly, secure it to the groove of the top cover plate using M 2.5*14 Phillips combination screws, rotate the Hall sensor clockwise to the lowest position, and tighten the screws (the Hall sensor should touch the bottom of the groove on the top cover plate, and the sensor bracket should touch the top cover plate).
After installing the sensor, reassemble the device in reverse order
Open the device casing
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

reference.label: Refer to IAS-3120 Disassembly and Assembly

steps.instruction: Use needle-nose pliers to loosen the optical fiber and unscrew one end by hand, paying attention to the light source connections on both sides
Use a screwdriver to remove the screws securing the other end of the optical fiber to the spectrum analyzer and remove the optical fiber
Reassemble in reverse order
